topic.what: Automated LLM red-teaming framework combining crowdsourced harmful queries and jailbreak tactics
topic.how: Neural Thompson Sampling contextual bandit with contrastive embeddings selects instruction compositions adaptively, balancing exploration-exploitation in combinatorial space of 50000×13000+ combinations
topic.why_matters: Existing approaches suffer from either limited semantic diversity (trial-and-error) or limited effectiveness (random combination); this bridges the gap for safety training data generation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of attacks that bypass target LLM safety as judged by Llama-Guard-2-8B evaluator
characteristics.vs_baseline: 25.2% WildTeaming baseline on Mistral-7B (+125% relative improvement)
characteristics.context: Aggressive bandit (λ=0.1); subtle bandit achieves 36.3% (+44% relative)
Shows model transfer robustness; Llama-3.3-70B achieves 55.8% (+205% relative)

characteristics.what_itasures: Count of distinct harmful queries selected throughout red-teaming
characteristics.context: Subtle bandit (high exploration) substantially surpasses WT in unique query count vs baseline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average cosine similarity between generated attacks and component queries
characteristics.vs_baseline: Subtle bandit matches WildTeaming; aggressive bandit higher
characteristics.context: Tradeoff: aggressive maximizes effectiveness, subtle maximizes diversity coverage

characteristics.vs_baseline: Higher λ = more exploration = more diverse but lower ASR
characteristics.context: Subtle bandit (λ=1.0) prioritizes exploration; aggressive (λ=0.01-0.1) prioritizes exploitation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Candidates scored per trial for bandit selection
characteristics.vs_baseline: Random would sample uniformly without scoring
characteristics.context: K=500 candidates per trial; many-armed bandit literature approach

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance consistency across different target LLMs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Improvements hold across Mistral-7B, Llama-3-70B, Llama-3.3-70B
characteristics.context: AIC outperforms WT on all three target models tested

requires: GPT-4 class attacker LLM for effectiveness
K>=500 candidates per trial for best performance

novelty: Combinatorial neural bandit for instruction composition in massive text space
Contrastive embedding + UMAP dimensionality reduction for scaling bandit to 8T+ instruction space
Joint optimization of effectiveness AND diversity simultaneously
Deduplication mechanism forcing generalization across semantic clusters
Subtle vs aggressive bandit exploration strategies for different safety objectives
open_problems: Multi-turn attack sequences (current approach is single-turn)
More nuanced diversity metrics beyond cosine similarity
Scaling to dynamic adversaries with defense adaptation
